report.summary: This storage account uses Geo-Redundant Storage (GRS) but has no replication requirement tags. Downgrading to LRS saves 33% while maintaining adequate durability for most workloads.

remediationScripts.description: Change storage replication from GRS to locally-redundant LRS
remediationScripts.azureCli: az storage account update --name stgarchivedata01 --resource-group rg-prod --sku Standard_LRS

recommendations: Verify if GRS replication is required by your compliance policy
Enable Azure Storage lifecycle management to auto-tier blobs
Consider archive tier for cold data older than 180 days

report.summary: This development VM (Standard_D8s_v3) is significantly oversized for typical dev workloads. A Standard_D2s_v3 would provide adequate performance at 50% lower cost.

remediationScripts.description: Resize development VM from D8s_v3 to D2s_v3
remediationScripts.azureCli: az vm resize --resource-group rg-dev --name dev-app-worker-04 --size Standard_D2s_v3
remediationScripts.azurePowerShell:
Stop-AzVM -ResourceGroupName rg-dev -Name dev-app-worker-04 -Force
$vm = Get-AzVM -ResourceGroupName rg-dev -Name dev-app-worker-04
$vm.HardwareProfile.VmSize = 'Standard_D2s_v3'
Update-AzVM -ResourceGroupName rg-dev -VM $vm
Start-AzVM -ResourceGroupName rg-dev -Name dev-app-worker-04

recommendations: Schedule auto-shutdown for nights and weekends (10 PM to 6 AM)
Consider Azure DevTest Labs for non-production VMs
Enable Azure Advisor right-sizing alerts for ongoing monitoring

report.summary: This static public IP is unattached and incurring unnecessary costs. Delete or attach to a resource immediately.

remediationScripts.azureCli: az network public-ip delete --resource-group rg-dev --name pip-ingress-frontend

recommendations: Run regular resource cleanup audits
Enable tagging policy to track resource ownership

report.summary: This PostgreSQL server uses General Purpose tier with 8 vCores – likely overkill for the observed workload. Consider downgrading to GP_Gen5_4 or switching to Burstable tier.

remediationScripts.description: Scale down PostgreSQL server from 8 vCores to 4 vCores
remediationScripts.azureCli: az postgres server update --resource-group rg-prod --name psql-customer-core --vcore 4 --sku-name GP_Gen5_4

recommendations: Review query performance metrics before downsizing
Enable auto-pause for Development/QA PostgreSQL Flexible Servers
Consider connection pooling to reduce vCore requirements

report.summary: This SQL database runs on expensive Premium P4 tier. Based on typical patterns, Standard S3 would suffice, saving approximately 70%.

remediationScripts.description: Downgrade SQL Database from Premium P4 to Standard S3
remediationScripts.azureCli: az sql db update --resource-group rg-prod --server sqldb-finance-reporting-server --name sqldb-finance-reporting --edition Standard --service-objective S3
remediationScripts.azurePowerShell: Set-AzSqlDatabase -DatabaseName sqldb-finance-reporting -ServerName sqldb-finance-reporting-server -ResourceGroupName rg-prod -Edition Standard -RequestedServiceObjectiveName S3

recommendations: Evaluate DTU usage over the last 30 days via Azure Metrics
Consider serverless tier for sporadically accessed databases
Enable automatic tuning recommendations

report.summary: This managed disk is orphaned – not attached to any VM. Delete immediately to stop incurring premium storage charges.
recommendations: Enable Azure Policy to block unattached premium disks
Implement regular disk cleanup runbooks

report.summary: This memory-optimized VM (E8s_v3) is deployed in Japan East – one of the most expensive Azure regions. Consider relocating to Southeast Asia or downgrading to E4s_v3 if 128 GB RAM is not required.

remediationScripts.description: Resize memory-optimized VM from E8s_v3 to E4s_v3
remediationScripts.azureCli: az vm resize --resource-group rg-prod --name prod-oracle-erp-db --size Standard_E4s_v3
remediationScripts.azurePowerShell:
Stop-AzVM -ResourceGroupName rg-prod -Name prod-oracle-erp-db -Force
$vm = Get-AzVM -ResourceGroupName rg-prod -Name prod-oracle-erp-db
$vm.HardwareProfile.VmSize = 'Standard_E4s_v3'
Update-AzVM -ResourceGroupName rg-prod -VM $vm
Start-AzVM -ResourceGroupName rg-prod -Name prod-oracle-erp-db

recommendations: Evaluate if Oracle workloads can run in Southeast Asia region (20% cheaper)
Consider Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) for Oracle-specific workloads
Review RAM utilization metrics before downsizing memory-optimized SKUs

report.summary: This Standard load balancer has no backend pool members and no health probes configured. It appears idle and is costing ~$16/month.

remediationScripts.description: Delete idle load balancer with empty backend pool
remediationScripts.azureCli: az network lb delete --resource-group rg-dev --name lb-int-dev-01

recommendations: Implement regular network resource audits
Set up alerts for load balancers with zero backend pool members
